parsed_review.summary: CP00-535 is a descriptor-only RP17 AI-governance closeout pack covering RP17.P05.M06.S19-RP17.P06.M02.S20 (risk class C, 150 units), verified through read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) across baseline, manifest, contract projection, registry bindings, service/validators source, tests, validation script, README, and the live next-pack queue. It is internally consistent at every layer: deliverable distribution {implementation:58, fixture:28, claude_review:7, security_audit:23, test:18, hermes_evidence:4, ui:12}=150 matches prompt/baseline/manifest/registry/validator; phase counts (P05:90 + P06:60) and the eight micro-phase row counts both sum to 150; CP00-535 is the legitimate successor to live-latest CP00-534 (registry CP534.next_pack_id=CP00-535, CP535.upstream_pack_id=CP00-534, next_subphase RP17.P06.M02.S21) with matching source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65 and deviation_from_plan:false. Descriptor-only boundaries are enforced everywhere (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false) plus the CP535 no-write attestation that spreads the CP514 base keeping all critical flags false (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, executes_command_runtime, loads_real_fixture_data, exposes_raw_payload, promotes_claude_to_final_approval, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation); a grep of packages/ai-governance/src and the validator script found no write/network/exec/db primitives. The pack does NOT over-claim: production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review; the Hermes production_ready_candidate:true reflects descriptor-coverage validity only and weakens no gate. Claude's read-only/non-final-approver and no-enterprise-trust-from-local-validation boundaries are asserted in the no-write attestation, descriptor authority_boundary (human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true), Claude review packet (read_only:true, tools Read/Grep/Glob), tests, and validator deepEqual checks. Hermes appears only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (H17), not product runtime. no_real_data:true across evidence packets with synthetic-data policy and no-leak guards enforced. CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte output, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero well-formed JSON and no invalid attempts. All 7 required CP535 public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and re-export via index.js; command-evidence.json records all required validation commands at exit_code 0; the modified progress-control-room files and untracked workbook/docx files are correctly classified as unrelated and excluded from scope; post-review artifacts (raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are correctly absent at this read_only_claude_run stage. No findings at any severity. As read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), I confirm the pack-level production_ready:false claim is well-supported and the boundaries hold; human final approval remains required for any downstream runtime opening.
